title: Explicit --build-arg causing all stages to ignore cache
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
When building a large Dockerfile, I noticed that `podman build` was failing to cache earlier layers. In this reproducer, passing `--build-arg` with the default value causes all of the build stages to be re-run.

However, it does *not seem* that the cache is invalidated; subsequent layers state that the cache was used.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Run `podman build . --build-arg="PYTHON_VERSION=3.8.5"` with this Dockerfile:
    ```
    FROM centos:centos7 as builder
    
    RUN echo "i am a no op $(date -u)"
    RUN echo "i am also a no op $(date -u)"
  	    
    ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.8.5
    RUN echo "I have been executed with [${PYTHON_VERSION}]"
    RUN echo "i am still a no op $(date -u)"

    ```

2. Run  `podman build . --build-arg="PYTHON_VERSION=3.8.5"` again

**Describe the results you received:**
The first build produces the following output: 
[out-0.txt](https://github.com/containers/buildah/files/6090290/out-0.txt)

The subsequent build produces this output instead: 
[out-1.txt](https://github.com/containers/buildah/files/6090291/out-1.txt)


**Describe the results you expected:**
In `out-1.txt`, I expected to see only cache messages. Instead the newly executed RUN command outputs are visible, with different date-times to the original build.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
* I'm using the Ubuntu 20.04 `.deb` from https://build.opensuse.org/package/live_build_log/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman/xUbuntu_20.04/x86_64
* This doesn't happen when using `buildah bud --layers`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.0.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @rhatdan should this not be left in containers/podman? I am unable to reproduce this bug with `buildah bud --layers`, only with `podman build`.

It also looks like https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3026 is related / the same issue. I've updated the reproducer in this issue to show that all layers are steps the cache (at least, all steps with RUN commands).

@agoose77 Are you sure `--build-arg` is even necessary to trigger this bug?

Testing with the following Dockerfile:
```
FROM ubuntu:18.04
ARG EXAMPLE=yesplease
RUN echo "Not using cache..."
```

for me `buildah bud --layers -f - < Dockerfile` will use caching as expected but `podman build --layers -f - < Dockerfile` will **always** execute the RUN command.

I'm running podman 3.0.1 and buildah 1.19.6 (Fedora 33) and I am quite sure that this behaviour is new and layer caching with ARG worked for me not too long ago.
I have since found that the build arg flag isn't required to trigger this bug.
This was a regression in buildah 1.19.4 and has since been fixed in buildah 1.19.6. Can you please try again with podman v3.1.0-rc1, which has the fixed buildah.
Since we believe this is fixed in the main branch, I am closing.

Thanks for the efforts everyone. I'm waiting on the suse repository for a pre-built package and then I can confirm, but it makes sense that this would be fixed - I hadn't realised that podman vendored (?) an older buildah.
@agoose77 please rename the issue to something like `Podman does not use cache`
I believe this issue may have regressed. I have been finding a lot of issues with caching in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15887

@rhatdan: [here](https://gitlab.com/han-aim/lern/-/jobs/3370942280#L48)'s a trace where the remote cache is somehow missed once `ARG` comes into play, even with a constant and default value across build runs.

I am having a similar problem, seems that I solved using the `--cache-to` and `--cache-from` argument.

For example

```
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:37

ARG BASH_COMPLETION_VER='2.11'

RUN dnf install -y "bash-completion-$BASH_COMPLETION_VER"

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=30s --retries=3 CMD pgrep bash
```

Gitlab CI pipeline

```yaml
---
stages:
  - publish

publish:
  stage: publish
  image:
    name: quay.io/podman/stable:v4.4.2
    entrypoint: [""]
  before_script:
    - podman login
          --username "$CI_REGISTRY_USER"
          --password "$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD"
          "$CI_REGISTRY"
  script:
    - podman build
          --layers
          --cache-to "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE"
          --cache-from "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE"
          --file "Dockerfile"
          --tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}"
          --tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}"
          --tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest"
          .
    - podman push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}"
    - podman push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}"
    - podman push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest"
```
